_comment: Config for blank_calc.py. All fields are dynamic - new colors that appear in the sales feed flow through automatically; only merges/exclusions/floors are declared here.

sizeCurves._comment: Named size distributions (percent). 'industry' is the standard screen-print curve (~1:2:3:3:1 S:M:L:XL:2XL). Add per-style curves under styleCurves to override.

styleCurves._comment: Optional per-style curve name override, e.g. { "Youth Shirt": "youth" }. Falls back to 'industry'.

colorAliases._comment: Map a sold color name to the blank color you actually order. Ash was a bulk-order color name; the orderable blank equivalent is Heather Gray.

_excludedSizes_help: Sizes to never order, dropped from BOTH curves and renormalized. For 'no 3XL or higher' list every such size, e.g. ["3XL", "4XL", "5XL"]. Leave [] to allow all sizes.

styleLabels._comment: Human labels for the canonical style keys the calculator derives (Unisex Shirt / Youth Shirt / Tank).

_manualHistory_help: Optional extra sales history to fold in alongside the feed. Each row: itemType (Shirt/Tank), style (UM/Y, or blank for tanks), color, size, unitsSold. Example: { "itemType": "Shirt", "style": "UM", "color": "Navy", "size": "L", "unitsSold": 3 }
_email_help: Optional fields for the email draft (recommended_order_<N>_email.md). Leave blank to use [placeholders] you fill in by hand.
